message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="Acknowledged: revised my atomicity position earlier">
Fair pushback, and you're right to draw that distinction between cohesive and atomic. I corrected my position in my second message — I agreed with Kent that this was several natural atomic steps compressed into one and listed 8 natural breakpoints I could identify. And yes, my CSS review would absolutely have been sharper if I'd been looking at 30 lines of component styles in isolation rather than 168 lines buried in a 6,500-line diff. Point taken and already internalized.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Steve Schoger is acknowledging my earlier pushback. No need to respond -- this is already resolved and he has given his consent on the action items.
